channel.title: NDTV News Search Records Found 1000
channel.description: NDTV.com provides the latest information from and in-depth coverage of India and the world. Find breaking news, India news, top stories, elections, politics, business, cricket, movies, lifestyle, health, live TV, videos, photos and more.

item.title: "Worthless, Devoid Of Credibility": Mojtaba Tears Into Trump Over Iran Deal

media:content.media:description: Earlier in the day, a negotiator said Iran was suspending its commitments to the interim deal

item.description: As renewed hostilities flared on Saturday, Iran's Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei vowed to teach the United States, who he referred to as Satan, "unforgettable lessons".
item.content:encoded: As renewed hostilities flared on Saturday, Iran's Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei vowed to teach the United States, who he referred to as Satan, "unforgettable lessons".

item.title: 2 US Soldiers Killed, 1 Missing After Iranian Strikes On Jordan

media:content.media:description: Pentagon said that service member is currently missing.

item.description: CENTCOM said it would not release further details, including the identities of the service members who died, until 24 hours after their relatives have been formally notified.
item.content:encoded: CENTCOM said it would not release further details, including the identities of the service members who died, until 24 hours after their relatives have been formally notified.

item.title: US-Iran War News LIVE: Iran's Supreme Leader In A New Statement Warns Of 'Unforgettable Lessons'

media:content.media:description: US-Iran War News LIVE: The US-Iran tensions have escalated in recent days

item.description: US-Iran War News Live Updates: Iran claimed it destroyed "several US refuelling aircraft and fighter jets" and caused "serious damage to many more".
item.content:encoded: US-Iran War News Live Updates: Iran claimed it destroyed "several US refuelling aircraft and fighter jets" and caused "serious damage to many more".

item.title: "Us, Blade, Your Throat": Iran Hardliners Turn On Leaders Amid Coup Claims

media:content.media:description: The anger on display at Khamenei's funeral has now spilt into Iran's political establishment

item.description: Hardliners argue that Iran's leaders are sidelining parliament, ignoring the supreme leader's instructions during negotiations with the US and weakening the country's revolutionary institutions.
item.content:encoded: Hardliners argue that Iran's leaders are sidelining parliament, ignoring the supreme leader's instructions during negotiations with the US and weakening the country's revolutionary institutions.

item.title: FIFA World Cup 2026 Cost Employers $11.7 Billion In Losses. Here's Why

media:content.media:description: The FIFA World Cup 2026 may have cost the US economy $11.7 billion in terms of lost productivity

item.description: The FIFA World Cup 2026 may have cost the US economy $11.7 billion in terms of lost productivity. Globally, the tournament may have led to a loss of $17 billion in productivity.
item.content:encoded: The FIFA World Cup 2026 may have cost the US economy $11.7 billion in terms of lost productivity. Globally, the tournament may have led to a loss of $17 billion in productivity.

item.title: Iran Demands "Blood For Blood" In New Billboard With Trump's Family In Coffins

media:content.media:description: The banner bears the slogan "Blood for Blood" in Persian

item.description: The banner, which has a burning White House as a backdrop, features Trump's five children: Ivanka, Don Jr, Eric, Tiffany, and Barron Trump; and the First Lady, Melania Trump, along with the US president.
item.content:encoded: The banner, which has a burning White House as a backdrop, features Trump's five children: Ivanka, Don Jr, Eric, Tiffany, and Barron Trump; and the First Lady, Melania Trump, along with the US president.

item.title: US Government Employees Now Allowed To Download TikTok On Official Devices

media:content.media:description: A 2022 law banned federal employees from using the app on government devices.

item.description: In January, TikTok said the venture will retrain, test and update TikTok's content recommendation algorithm on US user data.
item.content:encoded: In January, TikTok said the venture will retrain, test and update TikTok's content recommendation algorithm on US user data.

item.title: US Woman Mistook Pregnancy Symptoms For Medication Side Effects, Welcomes Baby Boy Unexpectedly
item.description: MacDougall recalled taking several pregnancy tests shortly before giving birth, and all of them came back negative.
item.content:encoded: MacDougall recalled taking several pregnancy tests shortly before giving birth, and all of them came back negative.

item.title: Iran Claims 2 Oil Tankers Exploded After Hitting Mines In Hormuz, US Denies

media:content.media:description: Iran Guards said that they had "stopped" four ships trying to transit the Strait of Hormuz.

item.description: Responding to the claim, the US Central Command (CENTCOM) said, "Like most IRGC claims, this is false," on X.
item.content:encoded: Responding to the claim, the US Central Command (CENTCOM) said, "Like most IRGC claims, this is false," on X.

item.title: 'Good Decision': Trump On FIFA Chief Reversing US Player's Suspension

media:content.media:description: It's a controversy Infantino and other FIFA officials likely want to move past.

item.description: Folarin Balogun's case became a flashpoint in the tournament, generating widespread criticism of FIFA's leadership and the US president.
item.content:encoded: Folarin Balogun's case became a flashpoint in the tournament, generating widespread criticism of FIFA's leadership and the US president.

item.title: How FBI Arrested Gangster Nitish Kaushal Metres Away From Canada Border

media:content.media:description: Nitish Kaushal was arrested on July 16 in Alburgh, Vermont.

item.description: Nitish Kaushal, linked to transnational crime, captured near Canada after abandoning tracked phone
item.content:encoded: Nitish Kaushal, linked to transnational crime, captured near Canada after abandoning tracked phone

item.title: "To Minimise Difficulties": India To Engage With US On Revised Visa Policies

media:content.media:description: India addresses difficulties faced by its citizens under new US visa rules.

item.description: India is communicating with US authorities to ease issues from new visa rules affecting Indian students and visitors.
item.content:encoded: India is communicating with US authorities to ease issues from new visa rules affecting Indian students and visitors.

item.title: Leather Jacket Worn By Nvidia CEO Auctions For Nearly $1 Million

media:content.media:description: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang gives a speech during Nvidia/Japan AI Ecosystem Reception in Tokyo on July 16.

item.description: A black leather jacket sported by Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang sold for $960,000 at a New York auction on Friday, 16 times higher than the estimated value of $60,000, according to Sotheby's.
item.content:encoded: A black leather jacket sported by Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang sold for $960,000 at a New York auction on Friday, 16 times higher than the estimated value of $60,000, according to Sotheby's.

item.title: US Hits Iran 7th Night In Row, Tehran Replies With "No Border Safe" Warning

media:content.media:description: US said that strikes against Iran were designed to "continue degrading Iranian military capabilities."

item.description: US forces launched strikes against Iran for a seventh night in a row on Friday, the US military said.
item.content:encoded: <iframe title="Player" class="head-story__img img__full lazyload" height="300" scrolling=no frameborder=no src="about:blank" data-src="https://www.ndtv.com/video/embed-player/id/1129228/play-via-app-wap?pWidth=100&pHeight=100&embed_type=story&site=classic&autostart=1"> </iframe>US forces launched strikes against Iran for a seventh night in a row on Friday, the US military said.

item.title: Elon Musk's SpaceX In Talks To Provide Computing Power To Pentagon: Report

media:content.media:description: Discussions between SpaceX and the Pentagon are ongoing and could still fall apart, WSJ reported.

item.description: Elon Musk's SpaceX is in talks to provide the US Department of Defense with access to data center capacity worth billions of dollars to run AI models, the Wall Street Journal reported on Friday, citing people familiar with the matter.
item.content:encoded: Elon Musk's SpaceX is in talks to provide the US Department of Defense with access to data center capacity worth billions of dollars to run AI models, the Wall Street Journal reported on Friday, citing people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Smoke, Fire, Floods: US Faces Triple Extreme Summer Weather Threats

media:content.media:description: Nearly 3.72 million acres (1.51 million hectares) have burned nationwide so far this year.

item.description: Wildfire smoke blanketed the eastern US from the Great Lakes to Washington, DC on Friday, floodwaters tore through Texas's Hill Country for a third day, and new fires erupted in the Pacific Northwest overnight, with 68 large blazes now burning in 15 states.
item.content:encoded: Wildfire smoke blanketed the eastern US from the Great Lakes to Washington, DC on Friday, floodwaters tore through Texas's Hill Country for a third day, and new fires erupted in the Pacific Northwest overnight, with 68 large blazes now burning in 15 states.

item.title: Venezuela Earthquake: Death Count Rises To More Than 5,000

media:content.media:description: The 7.2 and 7.5 magnitude quakes that struck on June 24 killed 5,069 people.

item.description: The twin earthquakes that struck Venezuela last month killed over 5,000 people, National Assembly chief Jorge Rodriguez posted on Telegram Friday.
item.content:encoded: The twin earthquakes that struck Venezuela last month killed over 5,000 people, National Assembly chief Jorge Rodriguez posted on Telegram Friday.

item.title: 'No Border Will Be Safe': Iran Warns Of Full-Scale Offensive Against US
item.description: Tehran is ready to resume "full-scale offensive operations" if US strikes against it continue for another two or three days, a senior military advisor to Iran's supreme leader warned Friday.
item.content:encoded: Tehran is ready to resume "full-scale offensive operations" if US strikes against it continue for another two or three days, a senior military advisor to Iran's supreme leader warned Friday.

item.title: "We Don't Want Them": Trump Set To Replace Immigrant Truckers With Veterans

media:content.media:description: Indian immigrants form a significant part of the US trucking workforce

item.description: Addressing the military investment summit, Trump said his administration was preparing to take what he called "historic action" against undocumented truck drivers.
item.content:encoded: Addressing the military investment summit, Trump said his administration was preparing to take what he called "historic action" against undocumented truck drivers.

item.title: UK Nationalises British Steel, Takes Control From China's Jingye

media:content.media:description: Nationalisation brings British Steel back into government ownership for the first time since 1988.

item.description: The country's last factory that can make steel from scratch faced imminent closure last year after Jingye said the plant in Scunthorpe, northern England, was no longer financially viable.
item.content:encoded: The country's last factory that can make steel from scratch faced imminent closure last year after Jingye said the plant in Scunthorpe, northern England, was no longer financially viable.
